findings.title: The reworked T3 CI still consumes the dead T2 security surface
findings.detail: The packet required the dead T2 boundary to be respected exactly, and the receipt claims T3 neither consumed nor depended on docs/security/**, internal/securitytest/**, or testdata/security/**. The live CI matrix still runs make test in every native lane, and make test is repo-wide go test ./..., which includes internal/securitytest. That package's tests/helpers load docs/security/readiness-manifest.json and walk docs/security plus testdata/security, so T3's supported-target evidence remains coupled to dead T2 artifacts outside T3's declared dependency boundary. Even though the suite is green today, the closure claim is overstated and the exact dead-T2 boundary in the packet is not met.

blocking_findings.title: The reworked T3 CI still consumes the dead T2 security surface
blocking_findings.detail: Every native T3 CI lane still executes repo-wide go test ./..., which includes internal/securitytest and its reads of docs/security and testdata/security, so the lane does not actually respect the packet's dead-T2 boundary.
